question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMapper's use of FixedCustomFeeMapper (via CustomFeeMapper) to observe a topic where fixed fee amounts are mapped with incorrect precision due to the mapFraction() rounding (HALF_UP, 9 decimal places), causing the fee history to appear reorganized with different values than actually recorded?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTopic() does not check whether the topic is deleted before returning its history, causing a deleted topic's last-known state to be returned as if it were current, reorganizing the apparent active history of the topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CommonMapper.mapTimestampRange() function — which adds 1 nanosecond to stakingPeriod and then adds SECONDS_PER_DAY * NANOS_PER_SECOND — to observe an off-by-one error in timestamp range boundaries that reorganizes the apparent time boundaries of topic history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of CORS restrictions on GET /api/v1/topics/{id} to perform cross-origin requests from a malicious web page, reading topic history data and using it to construct a misleading reorganized view of the topic's transaction history for other users?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the three service calls in getTopic() each independently open and close database connections, causing a scenario where connection pool exhaustion between calls results in one service returning stale data from a different connection's transaction isolation level, reorganizing the apparent history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter.valueOfNullable() logic — where a two-component ID assigns the first group to realm — to submit '0.1' and have it resolve to realm=0, num=1 (same as '0.0.1'), causing the same topic history to be accessible under multiple IDs and reorganizing the apparent uniqueness of topic history?
